title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for her former patient with Morquio syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Erin Donnelly, a physical therapist at Franciscan Children's, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her former patient Giovanni Thompson.
Giovanni "Gio" Thompson, who had Morquio syndrome, passed away at age 5, leaving a lasting impact on Donnelly.
Donnelly recalls Gio's inspiring approach to life, his love for Hulk, and a unique connection formed during his treatment.

title: Bruins fall to Devils 4-3 in OT as US Olympic hero Jack Hughes has 3 assists
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Bruins lose 4-3 to Devils in OT, hurting playoff chances as they hold final Eastern Conference wild-card spot.
Jack Hughes assists on three goals, reaching fastest 400 career points in Devils history; Devils' game-winner with 6.2 seconds left.
David Pastrnak scores twice for Bruins, who fail to secure consecutive wins for the first time since late January.

title: Tyngsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, of Tyngsborough, charged with murder after remains believed to be Jill Kloppenburg found under his garage.
Kloppenburg, 47, was reported missing in February 2025; her body believed to be discovered in a garage in Tyngsborough.
Police used radar technology to locate human remains under a patched area of Sullivan's garage floor.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman, 45, from Houston, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her son Charles and his Massachusetts General Hospital doctor.
Charles Felterman has Dup15q syndrome, causing epilepsy and developmental delays; Dr. Ronald Thibert at MGH has provided specialized care.
Felterman aims to raise funds for Massachusetts General Hospital, crediting their care with significantly improving her son's quality of life.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers, 35, from Belmont, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his wife, Coney, a leukemia survivor.
Coney Flowers was diagnosed with Acute Lymphoblastic leukemia in 2024 and underwent successful treatment, including a bone-marrow transplant.
Ben is fundraising for Caring for a Cure, praising their support during his wife's treatment and ongoing recovery journey.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie, 33, from Southampton, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Mass General's Pediatric Cancer team.
Participants in Labrie's run can honor individuals affected by pediatric cancer through donations to her cause.
Labrie's campaign, "I Run For Them," honors both children and adults affected by cancer and other conditions.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary 'Out of Body' premieres on Netflix April 13, exploring his life and creative journey.
Kahan's fourth studio album, 'The Great Divide,' will be released on April 24, shortly after the documentary.
The film covers Kahan's rise to fame, mental health challenges, and connection to his Vermont roots.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns to WBZ-TV as a 'special contributor' after being laid off last fall amid company buyout.
Keller will provide political analysis, moderate debates, and host 'Keller At Large' with Gov. Maura Healey as the first guest.
Keller, a 12-time Emmy winner, joined WBZ in 2005 following 14 years at WLVI-TV and continues writing for MASSterList.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers consider a bill to end biannual clock changes, moving permanently to Atlantic Standard Time.
83% of Boston.com readers in an informal poll support ending the clock changes, citing disruption to daily routines.
Opposition concerns include darker winter mornings and potential coordination issues with neighboring states.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham was arrested after a high-speed chase and crash in New Hampshire.
The teen drove a Chrysler 300 at 118 mph on I-95 before crashing into a wall on Route 101.
He faces multiple charges including felony reckless conduct, DUI, and possession of controlled drugs.
